purpose: Technical-first B1-B10 resolution program. Downstream translation is deferred until technical gates pass.

directions.technical_solution_target: Compiler method that reduces routed two-qubit work and exposure across external benchmarks while emitting replayable semantic certificates.
directions.current_evidence: 30/30 circuits; 481 virtual SWAPs removed; 37.18% 2Q reduction; 481/481 proof replay; post-virtual-SWAP 1Q resynthesis gives 1.018x logical T-count proxy; native Z-phase canonicalization gives a further 1.000990x; targeted control-RZ commuting removes 380 RZ gates and gives 1.119x logical T-count proxy over the native pass; U3 phase factoring now factors 1641 U3 gates, removes 313 additional RZ gates after factoring, emits 1641 factorization certificate events plus 3242 RZ-commute certificate events, passes 30/30 Aer cross-checks, and gives a further 1.036371x logical T-count proxy over control-RZ.
directions.main_blocker: No calibrated/live-like heavy-hex baseline; dynamic-circuit/reset/classical-control gaps; FT synthesis ledger re-costs sat_n11 as exact Clifford+T rotations and moves it to data-path dominance under parallel factories, but the new B7 min-STV boundary is gcm_h6 at 1.086008x; T-B7-004 shows explicit precision budgets do not clear 1.20x; T-B7-005 removes 0 arbitrary rotations; T-B7-006 shows repeated-angle cache reduces classical templates but not occurrence-based FT T ledger; T-B7-007 finds abundant repeated nonlocal templates but no adjacent inverse/duplicate block cancellation; T-B7-008/T-B7-009 find no exact four-arbitrary-angle w8_21 replacement across 43480 optimizer runs, and the claim-boundary fragment forbids counting repeated-template savings without an occurrence-removing certificate.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B1-004 or T-B7-010: either remove another 344 logical-T proxy from sat_n11, or produce a symbolic KAK/Clifford-scaffold proof or alternate certified rewrite for gcm_h6; stop expanding local w8_21 numerical searches without a new symbolic scaffold.

directions.technical_solution_target: Code/schedule/decoder comparison that beats surface-code target volume under the same confidence-bounded metric.
directions.current_evidence: Stim/PyMatching baseline: 30 configs, 90k shots; 40 Wilson target-volume combos; biased schedule proxy raised candidate-met targets from 22 to 28; real circuit-level operation-class sweep ran 90 configs / 270k shots and raises candidate-met targets from 22 to 26 with 0 volume-improvement rows; same-hardware reduced-round candidate ran 120 configs / 360k shots and found 22 Wilson target-volume improved rows, but the artifact boundary closes those rows as aggressive distance-3 one-round artifacts; T-B2-003 leakage-flagged analytic boundary found 42 proxy target-volume improved rows and 33 distance-5/7 rows; T-B2-004 Stim HERALDED_ERASE / DEPOLARIZE1 stress ran 108 configs / 216k shots and found 10 improved rows, all at candidate distance 5 or 7; T-B2-005 false-positive stress now runs 270 configs / 324k shots and 288 target comparisons, preserving 5 positive-false-positive d=5/d=7 improved rows at fp=0.001/tick but 0 improved rows by fp=0.003/tick.
directions.main_blocker: The reduced-round lever remains closed as a small-distance/aggressive-schedule artifact boundary. The heralded-erasure route now survives mild false-positive stress but fails by fp=0.003/tick, and it is still not a shot-conditioned erasure decoder, calibrated leakage model, threshold result, hardware result, or new-code claim.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B2-005: implement a shot-conditioned erasure decoder or calibrated leakage model, then rerun the d=5/d=7 target-volume comparison under noise mismatch and flag false-positive overhead.
directions.next_technical_experiment: Run T-B2-006: implement a real shot-conditioned erasure decoder or calibrated leakage model, then rerun the d=5/d=7 target-volume comparison under noise mismatch and flag false-positive overhead.

directions.technical_solution_target: Reaction-coordinate quantum resource estimate tied to observable error and classical baselines.
directions.current_evidence: PySCF small-molecule proxy plus B10-T1 reaction-coordinate denominators; B3 artifacts now run through T-B3-011. T-B3-011 covers H2/LiH/H2O/N2 with bounded high-coefficient sampled UCC/ADAPT pressure: 35 sampled groups total, 384 shots/group, mean/max relative variance error 0.0833/0.5029, max optimizer-loop shots lower bound 475,043,013,690,000, max optimizer-loop 2Q lower bound 281,225,464,104,480,000, and selected-CI larger-basis denominator wins remain 0.
directions.main_blocker: The current one-parameter UCC/ADAPT plus QWC route is demoted: it lacks converged multi-parameter chemistry, full compiled covariance, all-group sampling, and any advantage win, while optimizer-loop lower bounds are prohibitive.
directions.next_experiment: Keep B3 as a negative-boundary track unless T-B3-012 produces real multi-parameter UCCSD/ADAPT covariance or stronger-than-QWC measurement that beats selected-CI/DMRG/tensor denominators after optimizer-loop accounting.

directions.technical_solution_target: Circuit-level verifiable advantage task with explicit completeness/soundness against adaptive spoofers.
directions.current_evidence: Toy hidden-trap protocol rejects four spoofing families; shared B4/B8 CNOT hidden-projection refresh task runs 3 circuit-derived tasks / 192 configs with honest completeness 1.0, no-refresh high-leakage max soundness 0.675, and repaired high-leakage max soundness 0.0.
directions.main_blocker: The new task is still a CNOT/projection proxy, not a hardware-executable randomized-measurement protocol, hardness result, or quantum advantage claim.
directions.next_experiment: Upgrade the CNOT hidden-projection proxy to hardware-executable randomized measurement circuits or attack it with trained/generative spoofers.

directions.technical_solution_target: Hybrid quantum-tensor/embedding solver improving accuracy-per-resource on a meaningful correlated model.
directions.current_evidence: Exact small Hubbard plus cluster proxy; T-B5-001 oracle-tuned response denominator; T-B5-002 non-oracle response embedding denominator; T-B5-003a exact-state-seeded MPS/Schmidt pressure reference; T-B5-003b non-exact-state-seeded one-site MPS/ALS prototype; and T-B5-004 two-site finite-DMRG-style pressure prototype on the same 9 B10 D5 rows. The two-site prototype tests bond dimension 4 with 2 restarts x 4 sweeps, selected mean/max relative response error 0.08196/0.27710, beats one-site ALS on 4 rows, but beats exact-state-seeded MPS pressure on 0 rows.
directions.main_blocker: The two-site prototype improves over one-site ALS on some rows but is worse overall and still not canonical-environment production DMRG. The exact-state-seeded MPS pressure reference remains the strongest tensor denominator, so no B5 quantum or accuracy-per-resource route is open.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B5-005 / T-B10-013: replace the prototypes with mature canonical-environment variational DMRG/MPS, or compare a fully costed quantum impurity/response kernel against the D5 denominator ladder.

directions.technical_solution_target: Leakage-controlled high-Tc candidate ranking pipeline using physics descriptors, not family priors.
directions.current_evidence: Toy descriptor ranker plus T-B6-001 curated leakage audit, then T-B6-002 formula-derived descriptor screen: 38 records across 22 families, 12 expanded negative controls, embedded element-table descriptors, and B5-linked correlation/screening proxies. Formula AP@12 is 0.10 while family-prior AP@12 is 1.0; post-split formula AP is 0.5947 vs family-prior 0.9821. Validation errors 0, with explicit non-claim of material discovery, solved mechanism, complete database coverage, or computed quantum observable.
directions.main_blocker: Formula-derived descriptors expose a useful boundary but do not beat family-prior leakage. The table is still small, post-split negatives remain limited, and no crystallographic/DFT/B5-computed observables have been attached yet.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B6-003: replace formula-derived proxies with crystallographic, DFT, or B5-computed structural/electronic observables, then expand the post-2008 negative set so family priors and random baselines cannot saturate.

directions.technical_solution_target: End-to-end fault-tolerant resource ledger where B1/B2 improvements survive workload DAGs, factories, layout, and feed-forward.
directions.current_evidence: B1/B2 dependency bridge: 1.195x-1.616x conservative STV reduction; workload-DAG factory schedule: mean 1.475x; original logical T-factory schedule exposed a 1.0x min-STV boundary; control-RZ commute schedule improves min STV to 1.121951x; U3 phase-factored schedule preserves min STV at 1.121951x and improves mean STV to 1.234394x; min-STV classifier localizes the fixed-cost boundary to sat_n11; FT synthesis ledger re-costs sat_n11 from 294 to 262 T ledger units and gives 1.611481x STV under balanced/throughput factories, while exposing gcm_h6 as the new min row at 1.086008x; T-B7-004 shows precision-aware budgets do not clear 1.20x; T-B7-005 emits 172 local structural certificates and passes Aer 1/1, but arbitrary rotations remain 270 -> 270 and T ledger remains 6224 -> 6224; T-B7-006 shows 270 numeric occurrences collapse to 26 classical templates, but shared synthesis/cache removes 0 occurrence-based FT T-ledger units; T-B7-007 emits 2633 repeated-block certificates, with best template w8_21 covering 100 physical arbitrary occurrences, but finds 0 adjacent inverse/duplicate block pairs and removes 0 T ledger; T-B7-008/T-B7-009 test same-skeleton, exhaustive two-CNOT Rz/Ry, target-informed Euler-local, and bounded three-CNOT families for w8_21, totaling 43480 optimizer runs, 0 exact four-arbitrary-angle replacements, 0 arbitrary rotations removed, and 0 proxy-T ledger units removed.
directions.main_blocker: gcm_h6 remains factory-path dominated; precision-aware synthesis, conservative local same-axis merging, repeated-angle cache, configured nonlocal adjacent-block cancellation, and four tested w8_21 rewrite families all fail under the current physical occurrence-injection proxy. Repeated templates are now a catalog/compression signal, not a B7 physical resource claim, unless an occurrence-removing certificate is produced.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B7-010: either produce a symbolic KAK/Clifford-scaffold proof for w8_21, find a different certified gcm_h6 occurrence-removing rewrite, or return to B1 T-B1-004; do not count repeated-template savings without a verified rewrite.

directions.technical_solution_target: Classical verifier whose soundness stays low under adaptive leakage through challenge refresh/projection rotation.
directions.current_evidence: Honest completeness 1.0; five adversaries rejected; 0.75 leakage failure boundary; toy refresh/rotation repair makes high-leakage repair modes pass <=5% soundness; shared B4/B8 circuit-refresh task instantiates refresh/rotation on CNOT hidden-projection tasks and reduces high-leakage adaptive soundness from 0.675 without refresh to 0.0 under the best repaired modes; trained/generative spoofer stress runs 144 configs and shows no-refresh high leakage reaches learned soundness 1.0; B10-T2 proof-obligation gate rejects no-refresh high-leakage claims; restricted lemma gives a 8.94e-44 single-unknown-mask bound; transcript leakage simulator runs 192 configs; device-noise transcript bridge runs 480 configs and keeps challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation high-leakage soundness <=0.0208 under bounded noise; Qiskit/Aer verifier bridge instantiates 216 ideal randomized parity-verifier circuits with 0 semantic mismatches and honest completeness 1.0; noisy Aer bridge executes 9600 randomized parity-verifier circuits with circuit-level adversary inputs, bridge-safe noisy honest acceptance 1.0, and bridge-safe noisy adversary acceptance 0.0; backend-calibrated-style bridge derives per-qubit/per-edge Aer noise from Qiskit GenericBackendV2 target InstructionProperties and executes 5760 circuits with safe calibrated honest acceptance 1.0, safe calibrated adversary acceptance 0.25, and max honest predicate-bit error 0.0703125.
directions.main_blocker: The circuit-refresh, trained-spoofer, proof gate, restricted lemma, transcript simulator, device-noise bridge, and Aer bridges remain CNOT hidden-projection/proxy evidence. T-B10-008 now adds GenericBackendV2 calibration-style noise parameters, but it is still not real backend properties, hardware randomized-measurement verifier execution, cryptographic soundness, or sampling-hardness.
directions.next_experiment: Replace GenericBackendV2 snapshots with real backend properties or run a hardware randomized-measurement verifier, then compare against the noisy Aer, backend-calibrated-style, and transcript device-noise bridges.

directions.technical_solution_target: Formal restricted theorem or negative lemma about gap amplification/local-Hamiltonian hardness.
directions.current_evidence: 18-instance gap lab; 9 locality-preserving candidates; 0 accepted local candidates; finite-instance failed gap-amplification negative lemma with 4 strict width-trap counterexamples, 9 dense locality traps, and 5 proof obligations; Lean-style symbolic skeleton with 5 definitions, 3 theorem skeletons, and the same 5 open obligations; named-family cluster_stabilizer_open_uniform_reweight skeleton for n=4,5,6 showing all terms uniformly scaled by 1.35, max locality preserved at 3, raw gap amplified, normalized gap invariant, and the certificate rejected as global energy rescaling.
directions.main_blocker: The named-family statement is still not proof-assistant checked over all n; the current PATH `lean` command failed and is not a usable Lean/mathlib theorem-prover environment here. It is not a Quantum PCP proof, NLTS theorem, local-Hamiltonian hardness theorem, or global gap-amplification impossibility result.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B9-004: create a real Lean/mathlib or equivalent proof-checkable project and formalize support-size, uniform-scaling, spectral-width, and normalized-gap invariance lemmas for the open-boundary cluster-stabilizer family for all n >= 4.

directions.technical_solution_target: Formal theorem targets separating robust BQP advantage from fragile input/oracle/verification assumptions.
directions.current_evidence: 12-node/14-edge reduction graph; 2 formal theorem target cards; B10-T1 explicit-I/O accounting lemma, source-backed denominator note, D1/D2 and D5 denominator tables, B3/B5 denominator boundary comparison, T-B10-010 missing-assumption theorem note, T-B10-011 asymptotic access-contract note, and T-B10-012 B5 same-access sampling-or-DMRG bridge. T-B10-012 records 4 B5 denominator ladder rows, 5 sampling requirements that all block the current bridge, 6 seeded-MPS-over-non-oracle rows, 0 variational-over-seeded rows, no sampling oracle, no production DMRG, and no same-access positive route. B10-T2 remains a verifier proxy stack through backend-calibrated-style GenericBackendV2. B5/T-B5-004 now adds a two-site finite-DMRG-style pressure prototype that feeds the T-B10-013 production-DMRG gate: 4 rows beat one-site ALS, 0 rows beat exact-state-seeded MPS pressure, and no positive route is claimed.
directions.main_blocker: B10-T1 is now narrowed to a concrete B5 access-model blocker: no response-observable sampling oracle with variance/preparation costs exists, the strongest MPS pressure is exact-state seeded and not deployable, and the available variational MPS/ALS reference is nonproduction and loses to seeded pressure.
directions.next_experiment: Run T-B10-013 with T-B5-005: implement canonical-environment production DMRG/MPS for the same B5 Hubbard response rows, or propose a fully costed response sampler/quantum kernel strong enough to survive the T-B10-012/T-B5-004 denominator ladder; keep T-B10-009 for real backend verifier evidence.

downstream_translation_rule: No direction moves into publication, patent, financing, or productization as an active deliverable until it passes its technical gate.
